heading: ## Limitations
body:
- **Generalisability:** Single-centre design; results may not extend to other healthcare systems or patient populations.
- **Measurement validity:** PAM-13 is a proprietary instrument; its psychometric properties in the target language/culture are not yet validated.
- **Attrition risk:** 6-month follow-up may be insufficient to capture long-term activation changes; dropout rate unknown.
- **Technology dependence:** LLM-based intervention requires digital literacy and internet access; selection bias towards more activated patients.
- **Blinding:** Patients and clinicians cannot be blinded to group assignment; performance bias possible.
- **Sample size uncertainty:** σ placeholder (TBD) may underestimate true variability, reducing power.
- **Secondary outcomes multiplicity:** Bonferroni correction for ≤2 secondary endpoints may still inflate Type I error if exploratory analyses are added post hoc.
- **Interim analysis:** Futility-only stopping rule may not detect early harm beyond AE rate threshold.

heading: ## Consortium / partners
body:

- **Tbilisi State University (TSU)** — Biostatistics consulting (letter of support: pending)
- **University of Copenhagen (UCPH)** — Health economics analysis (letter of support: pending)

find:
- **Primary endpoint:** PAM-13 Δ ≥ 5.4 points (MCID)
- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025 for each of ≤2 secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ 55 per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)
- **Interim analysis:** Planned at N=100 (50 per group) for futility only (O'Brien-Fleming boundary, α-spending = 0.005). If conditional power <20%, trial will be stopped for futility.
- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary

- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025 for each of ≤2 secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ 55 per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)

- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary
- **Consistency note:** All falsifiability thresholds are identical across CONCEPT.md and THEORY.md. Any future updates must be synchronised across both files.

body: - **Consistency note:** The set of secondary outcomes listed here (MMAS-8, EQ-5D-5L, hospitalisations) must match exactly the set in THEORY.md. Currently, THEORY.md includes 'physician time per visit' as an additional secondary outcome; this discrepancy must be resolved before pre-registration.
